extr 0.9.0

Computes Confidence Intervals of Extinction Risk for a Density-Independent Population Model

confidence_interval_wz_di.Rd

Calculates the confidence interval for the extinction probability of a density-independent population model using the \(w\)–\(z\) method (Hakoyama, 2025).

Usage

confidence_interval_wz_di(mu, xd, s, th, tq, qq, alpha)

Arguments

mu

numeric: Estimated population growth rate, \(\hat{\mu}\).

xd

numeric: Distance to extinction threshold on a log scale, \(x_d = \log(n_q / n_e)\).

s

numeric: Estimated environmental variance, \(\hat{\sigma}^2\).

th

numeric: Time horizon for extinction probability evaluation, \(t_h\).

tq

numeric: Total observation period (from first to last data point), \(t_q\).

qq

integer: Number of time intervals (sample size minus 1), \(q\).

alpha

numeric: Significance level, i.e., \(\alpha\) = 1 - (confidence level).

Value

A numeric vector of length two containing the lower and upper confidence limits of the extinction probability.

Details

To improve extinction probability estimation, two transformed parameters, \(w\) and \(z\), are introduced as functions of the growth rate \(\mu\) and environmental variance \(\sigma^2\): $$ w = \frac{\mu t + x_d}{\sigma \sqrt{t}}, \quad z = \frac{-\mu t + x_d}{\sigma \sqrt{t}}, $$ where \(t\) is the time horizon and \(x_d = \log(n_q / n_e)\) is the log-distance to the extinction threshold.

The extinction probability within \(t\) is expressed as: $$ \Pr[T \leq t] = G(w, z) = \Phi(-w) + \exp\left(\frac{z^2 - w^2}{2}\right) \Phi(-z), $$ where \(\Phi(\cdot)\) is the standard normal cumulative distribution function.

The maximum likelihood estimators \(\widehat{w}\) and \(\widehat{z}\) follow non-central \(t\)-distributions with \(q-1\) degrees of freedom: $$ \widehat{w} \sqrt{\frac{q-1}{q}} \sqrt{\frac{t_q}{t}} \sim t(\delta_w, q-1), \quad \widehat{z} \sqrt{\frac{q-1}{q}} \sqrt{\frac{t_q}{t}} \sim t(\delta_z, q-1), $$ with drift parameters $$ \delta_w = w \sqrt{\frac{t_q}{t}}, \quad \delta_z = z \sqrt{\frac{t_q}{t}}. $$

Asymptotically exact \((1-\alpha)\) confidence intervals for \(w\) and \(z\) are obtained by numerically solving the non-central \(t\) quantile equations corresponding to the observed statistics.

The confidence interval for the extinction probability \(G(w, z)\) is then approximated by combining these intervals as $$ \left( G(\overline{\Psi_w}, \underline{\Psi_z}), \quad G(\underline{\Psi_w}, \overline{\Psi_z}) \right), $$ where \(\overline{\Psi_w}\), \(\underline{\Psi_w}\), \(\overline{\Psi_z}\), and \(\underline{\Psi_z}\) are the upper and lower confidence limits for \(w\) and \(z\), respectively.

This w-z method yields exact confidence intervals when \(z \gg 0\) and robust approximations when \(z \ll 0\).

Author

Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com
